title: Agentic Prompt-Injection Ontology
description: A multi-axis ontology for classifying prompt-injection and agentic attacks. Explicitly extends OWASP LLM01, MITRE ATLAS AML.T0051, and the HackAPrompt taxonomy. New nodes are flagged with 'extension': true.

extends.note: Defines direct vs indirect prompt injection; does not enumerate delivery vectors beyond user-prompt and retrieved content, and does not classify techniques or goals beyond a high level.

related: AML.T0040 — ML Model Inference API Access
extends.note: Provides attacker-perspective technique IDs; delivery-vector and goal dimensions are under-specified for multi-agent pipelines.

extends.citation: Schulhoff et al., 'Ignore This Title and HackAPrompt: Exposing Systemic Vulnerabilities of LLMs through a Global Scale Prompt Hacking Competition', EMNLP 2023
extends.note: Richest technique taxonomy available from empirical competition data. Focused on direct, user-turn injection in single-turn pipelines; does not cover indirect, agentic, multi-hop, or tool-mediated vectors.

injection_type.description: Whether the adversarial instruction reaches the model directly from the attacker or indirectly through a trusted channel the model consumes.

direct.label: Direct Prompt Injection
direct.description: Attacker controls the user-turn prompt; instruction is injected without an intermediary.

indirect.label: Indirect Prompt Injection
indirect.description: Adversarial instruction is embedded in content the model retrieves or processes (documents, web pages, tool outputs, emails, memory).

multi_hop.label: Multi-Hop / Cross-Agent Injection
multi_hop.description: Injection propagates across two or more agents; an upstream agent is compromised and its output re-injects a downstream agent in the same pipeline.
multi_hop.extension_rationale: Existing frameworks treat injection as a single-hop event. Multi-agent orchestration creates transitive trust chains where one compromised agent can re-inject peers. This node is new.

delivery_vector.description: The channel through which the injected instruction reaches the model's context window.
delivery_vector.source_partial: OWASP LLM01 names 'user input' and 'retrieved content'; MITRE ATLAS does not enumerate vectors. All agentic vectors (DV-04 through DV-10) are extensions.

user_prompt.label: User Prompt
user_prompt.description: Injection arrives in the human-turn message sent directly to the model.

retrieved_document.label: Retrieved Document (RAG)
retrieved_document.description: Injection is embedded in a document fetched via retrieval-augmented generation.

web_content.label: Web Page / Fetched URL
web_content.description: Injection is hidden in a web page the agent visits (invisible text, Unicode tricks, steganography).

tool_output.label: Tool / API Output
tool_output.description: Injection is embedded in the structured or unstructured response from a tool the agent calls (search, calculator, code interpreter, database query).
tool_output.extension_rationale: Tool-use pipelines (function calling, ReAct, OpenAI Assistants) create a new surface not present in chat-only threat models.

memory.label: Agent Memory / Long-Term Store
memory.description: Injection is written into the agent's persistent memory in one session and retrieved in a future session, enabling deferred/sleeper attacks.
memory.extension_rationale: Memory-enabled agents (MemGPT, OpenAI memory, custom vector stores) create a new persistence surface with no equivalent in existing frameworks.

email_message.label: Email / Messaging Content
email_message.description: Injection arrives in an email or chat message that an agent with mailbox access reads and processes.
email_message.extension_rationale: Email-integrated agents are now common (Gmail Copilots, Outlook AI) but not enumerated as a distinct vector in existing frameworks.

file_upload.label: Uploaded File
file_upload.description: Injection is embedded in a PDF, Word document, spreadsheet, image EXIF, or other file the agent is asked to parse.
file_upload.extension_rationale: File parsing by agents (code interpreters, document processors) is a distinct attack surface from general RAG retrieval.

agent_message.label: Upstream Agent Output
agent_message.description: In a multi-agent system, a compromised or spoofed upstream agent's output carries the injection into the downstream agent's context.
agent_message.extension_rationale: Unique to multi-agent architectures. Enables the IT-03 multi-hop injection type. Not present in any reviewed framework.

system_prompt_leak.label: System Prompt / Context Manipulation
system_prompt_leak.description: Attacker manipulates or forges the system prompt (e.g., through prompt template injection, insecure direct object reference to templates, or chain-of-thought framing).

multimodal.label: Multimodal Content (Image / Audio / Video)
multimodal.description: Injection is embedded in image pixels, audio, or video frames processed by a multimodal model — e.g., adversarial text in an image, instructions in audio.
multimodal.extension_rationale: Multimodal models (GPT-4V, Gemini, Claude 3) create visual and auditory injection surfaces not covered by text-only frameworks.

technique.description: The rhetorical or structural mechanism used to make the model follow injected instructions.
technique.source_partial: HackAPrompt taxonomy is the primary empirical source for techniques. Agentic techniques (TQ-10 through TQ-14) are extensions.

context_ignoring.label: Context Ignoring
context_ignoring.description: Instructs the model to disregard prior system instructions and act on new ones. Classic 'ignore all previous instructions' pattern.

payload_splitting.label: Payload Splitting / Token Smuggling
payload_splitting.description: Splits the adversarial payload across multiple turns, tokens, or encoding boundaries so no single chunk triggers a filter.

obfuscation_encoding.label: Obfuscation / Encoding
obfuscation_encoding.description: Encodes the injection in Base64, hex, Unicode homoglyphs, leetspeak, invisible characters, or other transformations that evade surface-level filters.

roleplay_persona.label: Role-Play / Persona Adoption
roleplay_persona.description: Asks the model to adopt a persona or enter a fictional frame in which its safety constraints do not apply ('you are DAN', 'pretend you are an unrestricted AI').

refusal_suppression.label: Refusal Suppression
refusal_suppression.description: Explicitly instructs the model not to refuse, apologize, or add caveats. Often combined with other techniques.

few_shot_priming.label: Few-Shot Priming
few_shot_priming.description: Provides fabricated examples of the model complying with policy-violating requests to establish a behavioral prior for the current request.

completion_compliance.label: Completion Compliance
completion_compliance.description: Exploits the model's text-completion instinct by starting a harmful sentence and relying on the model to continue it naturally.

distractor.label: Distractor / Misdirection
distractor.description: Embeds the injection in surrounding benign text to obscure intent from content filters or human reviewers.

authority_spoofing.label: Authority / System Spoofing
authority_spoofing.description: Claims to be a privileged entity (the developer, the system prompt, a super-user, an internal tool) to override safety constraints.

tool_call_injection.label: Tool-Call Injection
tool_call_injection.description: Crafts content that causes the agent to emit malformed or adversarial tool calls (e.g., SQL injection via LLM → database connector, or SSRF via LLM → HTTP tool).
tool_call_injection.extension_rationale: Unique to tool-using agents. The injection exploits the model's code-generation behavior to produce dangerous downstream calls. Not covered by HackAPrompt (single-turn) or OWASP LLM01 at this granularity.

memory_poisoning.label: Memory Poisoning
memory_poisoning.description: Writes adversarial content into the agent's persistent memory so it is automatically retrieved and executed in future sessions without further attacker access.
memory_poisoning.extension_rationale: Enabled by memory-augmented agents. Creates a deferred, persistent attack surface analogous to a stored XSS. Not present in any reviewed framework.

goal_hijacking.label: Goal / Objective Hijacking
goal_hijacking.description: Replaces the agent's current task objective with an attacker-chosen goal. Differs from context-ignoring in that it operates at the planning/task level, not the turn level.
goal_hijacking.extension_rationale: Planning agents (AutoGPT, LangChain agents, OpenAI Assistants with instructions) maintain a goal state that can be overwritten by injected instructions mid-task.

chain_of_thought_manipulation.label: Chain-of-Thought Manipulation
chain_of_thought_manipulation.description: Injects reasoning steps into the model's chain-of-thought scratchpad to steer conclusions toward attacker-desired outputs, exploiting the model's tendency to follow its own prior reasoning.
chain_of_thought_manipulation.extension_rationale: CoT and scratchpad-reasoning models (o1, Claude extended thinking, ReAct) expose reasoning traces that can be manipulated. No existing framework addresses this surface.

trust_boundary_escalation.label: Trust-Boundary Escalation
trust_boundary_escalation.description: Exploits implicit trust between components (e.g., an agent trusting another agent's output without re-validating, or a tool result being treated as a system-level instruction).
trust_boundary_escalation.extension_rationale: Multi-agent and tool-use pipelines establish implicit trust hierarchies. Attacking trust boundaries is distinct from any single-turn technique.

goal.description: The attacker's intended outcome from a successful injection.
goal.source_partial: OWASP LLM01 names data-exfil and unauthorized actions; MITRE ATLAS focuses on societal harm and model access. Resource-abuse, agent-persistence, and supply-chain-poisoning are new.

data_exfiltration.label: Data Exfiltration
data_exfiltration.description: Extract confidential information: system prompt, user data, credentials, retrieved documents, memory contents.

unauthorized_action.label: Unauthorized Action
unauthorized_action.description: Cause the agent to take an action not sanctioned by the user or operator (send email, execute code, delete records, make API calls, place orders).

privilege_escalation.label: Privilege Escalation
privilege_escalation.description: Gain access to capabilities or data beyond the attacker's authorization level — e.g., reading admin data via an agent with elevated permissions.

policy_evasion.label: Policy / Safety Evasion
policy_evasion.description: Bypass content filters, usage policies, or safety guardrails to obtain an otherwise-refused response.

harmful_content.label: Harmful Content Generation
harmful_content.description: Cause the model to produce CSAM, extremist propaganda, bioweapons instructions, targeted harassment, or other directly harmful output.

resource_abuse.label: Resource Abuse (Compute / Cost / Rate-Limit)
resource_abuse.description: Cause the agent to make excessive tool calls, API requests, or compute-heavy operations to inflict cost on the operator or exhaust rate limits.
resource_abuse.extension_rationale: Agentic pipelines with looping and tool-use create a new economic attack surface. A compromised agent can be made to call expensive APIs in a loop. Not present in existing frameworks.

agent_persistence.label: Agent Persistence / Sleeper Payload
agent_persistence.description: Establish a persistent foothold in the agent's memory or configuration so that future sessions re-activate the attack without further attacker input.
agent_persistence.extension_rationale: Memory-augmented agents enable attacker persistence across sessions. Analogous to malware persistence but not addressed by existing AI security frameworks.

reputation_damage.label: Reputation / Brand Damage
reputation_damage.description: Cause the agent to produce defamatory, embarrassing, or policy-violating outputs attributed to the operator or their brand.

supply_chain_poisoning.label: Supply-Chain / Downstream Poisoning
supply_chain_poisoning.description: In a multi-agent or RAG pipeline, compromise one component so its outputs poison downstream agents, knowledge stores, or fine-tuning datasets.
supply_chain_poisoning.extension_rationale: Multi-hop injection chains and RAG pipelines create poisoning opportunities that extend well beyond a single interaction.

severity.description: Blast radius and reversibility dimensions for prioritization.
severity.note: Severity is a composite of impact scope, reversibility, and detection difficulty. It is assessed per incident, not per ontology node.

detection_difficulty.description: How hard is it for a human reviewer or automated system to detect this attack post-hoc?
detection_difficulty.extension_rationale: Existing frameworks do not enumerate detection difficulty as a first-class severity dimension. Agentic attacks (especially memory-poisoning and multi-hop) score high here.

latency.description: Whether the attack effect is immediate (this session), deferred (future session), or persistent (until memory/config is cleared).
latency.extension_rationale: Memory-based and sleeper attacks introduce deferred and persistent effect latency not represented in single-turn threat models.
